name: getSignalDescriptionForValue
title: getSignalDescriptionForValue
syntax: long getSignalDescriptionForValue(Signal signal, int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 1
long getSignalDescriptionForValue(char signalNamel[], int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 2
long getSignalDescriptionForValue(serviceSignalNumber signal, int64 rawValue, char buffer[], long bufferSize); // form 3
description: Retrieves the description for the value of a database signal. In this way, you can access the value table of the signal.

parameters.name: rawValue
parameters.description: The value for which the description shall be retrieved. This must be the raw value, not the physical value.

parameters.name: buffer
parameters.description: String buffer which receives the value description.

parameters.name: Note Signal Ambiguity You have to use further objects to identify the signal uniquely. They are: channel, database name (alias), node name and message name. The exact qualification syntax is:[Channel::][Database name (alias)::][Node::][Message::]Signal The order and completeness of the objects from right to left is important (see example).

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
